- Bottom grid: (52,44)
The goal seems to be to move all green cells to the same column, like in level 1. In level 1, the goal was to have all green cells at column 42. In level 2, the green cells are at columns 14, 20, 32, 44, 44.
Let me check the segmentation to understand the layout better.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Analyze level 2
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("All nodes:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    cr = sum(p[0] for p in b)//len(b)
    cc = sum(p[1] for p in b)//len(b)
    print(f"  {n['color']}: center=({cr},{cc}), pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Select (16,14) and check for g overlay
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 16, 'col': 14}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('G', 'g', 'N'):
        b = n['boundary']
        cr = sum(p[0] for p in b)//len(b)
        cc = sum(p[1] for p in b)//len(b)
        print(f"{n['color']}: center=({cr},{cc})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
